item.title: Hiring more SDRs (evergreen)
item.description:
"We're hiring 5 more SDRs."
I keep hearing this. The companies actually pulling ahead are doing the opposite.
Look at the pattern: .. Block cut 40% of its team. Guided profit up 54%. .. Salesforce cut support headcount almost in half. Agentforce hit $500M ARR in one year. .. Klarna went from 5,500 to 3,400 employees. AI doing the work of 700 agents.
36% of B2B companies cut their SDR team. Highest cut rate of any sales role.
The shift isn't "fire everyone." It's "stop backfilling. Let the AI layer absorb the repetitive work. Keep the humans on the closing conversations."
If you're adding headcount to solve a pipeline problem, you're probably solving it with last decade's tools.
What's the smallest team that could run your entire sales motion today?
